channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
item.description: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.
item.content:encoded:
Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: A day after Alito's testy response to Sotomayor's dissent, court says it was a 'misunderstanding'
item.description: During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.
item.content:encoded:
During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: She posted about ICE. Five months later, DHS agents told her to take her post down
item.description: In January, Paigelynne Gonyea posted about the immigration surge in Minneapolis. This week, she was visited by ICE officials who claimed one of her posts had doxxed a federal agent.
item.content:encoded:
In January, Paigelynne Gonyea posted about the immigration surge in Minneapolis. This week, she was visited by ICE officials who claimed one of her posts had doxxed a federal agent.

(Image credit: Gracious Golden via Facebook/Screenshot by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: World Cup fans are missing games after their resale tickets fall through
item.description: The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.
item.content:encoded:
The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.

(Image credit: Shafkat Anowar)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: John Bolton, former Trump national security adviser, pleads guilty in classified documents case
item.description: Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."
item.content:encoded:
Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."

(Image credit: Alex Kent)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: 'We are with you, Venezuela': Houston community rallies after deadly earthquakes
item.description: Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.
item.content:encoded:
Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.

(Image credit: Lucio Vasquez)
item.dc:creator: Lucio Vasquez

item.title: How well do you know your Reflecting Pool news? Because the quiz will test you
item.description: This week, the beleaguered body of water faced new woes. Plus soccer, gambling and U.K. politics!
item.content:encoded: This week, the beleaguered body of water faced new woes. Plus soccer, gambling and U.K. politics!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Trump axed a Black history exhibit. Former park rangers are teaching it anyway.
item.description: As the U.S. prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, former national park rangers are hosting teach-ins and sharing history that the Trump administration has sought to erase from federal land.
item.content:encoded:
As the U.S. prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, former national park rangers are hosting teach-ins and sharing history that the Trump administration has sought to erase from federal land.

(Image credit: KT Kanazawich for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marissa J. Lang

item.title: Organizers say the Great American State Fair is for everyone. Some disagree
item.description: The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.
item.content:encoded:
The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.

(Image credit: Andrew Leyden)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: In a first since Trump deployed the guard to D.C., Democrats are sending troops
item.description: Several Democratic governors have sent National Guard troops to Washington, D.C., ahead of America 250, amid President Trump's highly visible — and controversial — ongoing deployment to the city.
item.content:encoded:
Several Democratic governors have sent National Guard troops to Washington, D.C., ahead of America 250, amid President Trump's highly visible — and controversial — ongoing deployment to the city.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf
